data.title: Exelixis shuttering Pennsylvania site, laying off 130 employees
data.description:
• Exelixis (NASDAQ:EXEL) is shutting down a facility in King of Prussia, Pa., to focus its operations at its headquarters in Alameda, Calif.
  • As a result, 130 employees will be laid off, according to Fierce Biotech.
  • "Exelixis hired a significant number of employees—including many in fully remote positions—during the pandemic and post-pandemic period to meet the company’s needs at the time," a company spokesperson told the website. "As our business has continued to evolve, we have made the difficult decision to reorganize the company’s structure to focus largely on our operations in Alameda, California."
  • The biotech is cutting other positions as well. A recent California Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) notice stated that 71 employees at headquarters are losing their jobs, along with another 33 remote employees.
  • In January 2024, Exelixis cut about 175 jobs.

data.title: Ideaya Biosciences gets coverage initiation at Barclays and Citizens
data.description:
Ideaya Biosciences (NASDAQ:IDYA) got coverage initiation from Barclays and Citizens, reflecting increasing confidence in the company’s oncology pipeline.

Barclays launched with an Overweight rating and a $40 price target, while Citizens assigned a Market Outperform rating and a slightly higher $41 price target. 

Citizens views the company’s lead program, darovasertib, as the foundation of its investment thesis and a value-defining near-term catalyst, noting pivotal phase 2/3 progression-free survival data in metastatic uveal melanoma expected by year-end 2025. 

"Near term investor focus on IDYA centers on its expansion into nextgenerationoncology modalities building on validated biology, and we believe these two readouts represent near-term inflection points that helps further derisk Ideaya's path to a meaningful commercial opportunity," Barclays analysts said.

While both Barclays and Citizens note risks, they have confidence on a positive outlook for the company.

IDYA shares up about 1% today.

data.title: Shell weighs sale of $3B stake in Australia LNG plant - Bloomberg
data.description:
Shell (NYSE:SHEL) is considering a sale of its 16.67% stake in the A$34B (~US$22B) North West Shelf liquefied natural gas export plant in Western Australia, which could be worth more than $3B, Bloomberg reported Thursday.

While Shell (NYSE:SHEL) is expanding its LNG business globally as it believes gas demand will continue to increase in the coming decades, the company is looking to exit North West Shelf due to its planned transition into a third-party tolling facility, a model that does not fit with the company's wider strategy and portfolio, according to the report.

North West Shelf operator Woodside Energy (WDS) has been consolidating its holdings of the asset, but the company has struggled in the past to get partners aligned on the strategy; Chevron last year sold its stake in the facility to Woodside, giving the operator a 50% share of the venture.

data.title: ‘Periodic corrections are welcome, guarding against complacency’ – DB’s Adrian Cox
data.description:
Hawkish investors are on edge looking for signs of a soft landing in the AI sector (AIEQ), (ARTY), ready to pounce on any indication that the AI boom may be overhyped or coming to an end, according to Deutsche Bank.

“The summer provided several opportunities for market corrections, enhanced by low liquidity and underlying concerns about the economy and interest rates,” said Adrian Cox, thematic strategist with Deutsche Bank.

Shares in AI-related stocks including Dell (DELL), Arm Holdings (ARM), AMD (AMD) and Broadcom (AVGO) fell last Friday after a series of good-but-not-good enough earnings reports.

Even industry giant Nvidia (NVDA) fell 3.3% after guidance that fiscal third-quarter revenues would be slightly short of the most bullish analysts’ expectations. Similarly, chipmaker Marvell (MRVL) experienced a substantial 19% drop after its guidance fell short of expectations.

Web searches for AI are now ten times higher than they ever were for crypto, according to Google Trends data cited in the research. Despite this clear evidence of market hype, with S&P 500 (SP500) companies mentioning AI more than 3,300 times in their recent earnings calls, valuations still appear more reasonable than during the dot-com bubble, said Cox.

 

 

 

“OpenAI CEO Sam Altman himself has said that investors are ‘over excited’,” he added, while emphasizing that current price-to-earnings ratios remain far below the extreme levels seen during previous tech bubbles.

The top four hyperscale cloud companies – Amazon (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOG), (GOOGL) and Meta (META) – are set to invest nearly $400B in AI capital expenditure this year, approximately equivalent to the GDP of Malaysia or Egypt, Cox said.

These major tech players can afford such massive investments because they are generating double-digit annual increases in revenues, with the second calendar quarter marking “an inflection point as together they earned an additional $5.6bn, up around 35 percent.”

But the path forward is not without challenges to sustained growth. Nvidia’s (NVDA) dependence on just a handful of customers presents a vulnerability, with its two largest clients accounting for 39% of its revenues in the most recent quarter.

“Periodic corrections are welcome, releasing some steam from the system and guarding against complacency,” Cox concluded, suggesting that recent pullbacks might actually contribute to healthier long-term market conditions.

data.title: Ciena soars after Q3, outlook surpasses estimates
data.description:
Shares of Ciena (NYSE:CIEN) jumped about 21% on Thursday after fiscal third quarter results and outlook exceeded expectations.

The networking systems provider saw third-quarter revenue grow 29.4% year-over-year to $1.22B, while adjusted EPS surged 91.4% year-over-year to $0.67. Both top and bottom line numbers beat estimates.

"We delivered another strong quarterly performance that reflects our leadership in high-speed connectivity and ongoing accelerated customer demand as the network becomes fundamental to the underpinning, growth, and monetization of AI," said Ciena's President and CEO Gary Smith.

The company said two customers represented 10%-plus of revenue combining for a total of 28.8% of revenue.

Accounts receivable, net balance was $1.03B.

Outlook

Ciena expects fiscal fourth quarter 2025 revenue to be between $1.24B and $1.32B (with midpoint at $1.28B) compared to consensus revenue estimate of $1.21B.

"With visibility well into fiscal year 2026, we are confident in the continued momentum of our business and remain focused on further expanding our operating leverage as we continue to grow," said Smith.

The company expects fourth quarter adjusted gross margin to be between 42% and 43%, and adjusted operating expense in a range of $390M to $400M.

Ciena expects fiscal 2026 revenue growth of about 17% year-over-year, while fiscal 2026 gross margins are expected to be 43% plus or minus 1 point.

The company expects its operating margin goal to 16% from 15% by 2026, accelerated by one year.

Update: The story was updated with more information on the company's guidance and stock price movement.

data.title: ISM Services Index rises more than expected in August
data.description: The U.S. ISM Services Index rose to 52.0 in August from 50.1 in July, exceeding the 50.9 consensus, according to data released by the Institute for Supply Management on Thursday.

data.title: Adnoc says EU probe delays could jeopardize Covestro deal - reports
data.description:
Abu Dhabi’s Adnoc said the European Union competition regulator's decision to temporarily halt its probe into the Covestro (OTCPK:CVVTF) deal could jeopardize the transaction. 

“While we remain committed to pursuing a constructive path forward, the continuation of such an approach raises serious questions about the viability of this investment,”  a spokesperson for XRG, the international investment arm of Adnoc,  told Reuters and MLex in an email. 

The European Commission was set to decide on the probe by Dec. 2, though it will now set a new deadline.  

"We are deeply disappointed by today's decision," the spokesperson for XRG told the news outlets. "The Commission's demands have strayed far beyond what is reasonable or relevant to this transaction, crossing into areas that are both disproportionate and invasive."

Covestro shares fell as much as 11% on Thursday in Frankfurt.

The European Commission in late July announced it was starting an in-depth probe into Abu Dhabi National Oil Co.'s €11.7B (~$13.75B) acquisition of Covestro (OTCPK:CVVTF) (OTCPK:COVTY) under the European Union's tough new foreign subsidy rules.  The EC said it will investigate concerns that Adnoc's state funding by the United Arab Emirates could allow it to behave in a way that interferes with fair competition across the bloc.

data.title: Silvaco Group appoints new CFO
data.description:
• Silvaco Group (NASDAQ:SVCO) has named Chris Zegarelli as its new chief financial officer, effective September 15, 2025.
  • Zegarelli most recently served as SVP of Finance at Infineon Technologies, where he led integration after its $830M acquisition of GaN Systems, where he was previously CFO.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: American Eagle Outfitters soars to 8-month high as Sydney Sweeney ad sweetens results
data.description:
American Eagle Outfitters (NYSE:AEO) stock hit a nearly eight-month high on Thursday after the apparel maker said the advertising campaign starring actress Sydney Sweeney gave its brand and business a reset.

The apparel company has seen a fair bit of controversy recently owing to the Sweeney ad. The “Sydney Sweeney has Great Genes Jeans” ad generated a lot of debate on both sides of the political aisle, with some accusing the company of endorsing eugenics and white supremacy, while others considered the public outcry “cancel culture run amok.” 

The company soared 31.8% to ~$18 as of 9:37 am ET, touching its highest levels since December 10, when it touched $18.09.

For the reported quarter, the apparel retailer earned a profit of $0.45 per share, more than double what Wall Street expected and 15% better than a year ago. Sales were down 1% to $1.28B but exceeded estimates by $40M.

For the current and fourth quarter, American Eagle (NYSE:AEO) expects to see gross margin decline from the same quarters last year, while operating income is expected to be ~$54M for Q3 and $56M for Q4.

For FY25, gross margin will also decline year-over-year, with operating income within a range of $255M to $265M.

The Relative Strength Index (RSI) for American Eagle Outfitters (AEO), calculated over the last 14 days and smoothed across a 250-day period, is 61.5. An RSI reading above 70 is considered an overbought level. 

The firm's price-to-earnings ratio for 2026, based on Seeking Alpha's consensus earnings estimates, stands at 12.22. 

Seeking Alpha analysts and quants rate the company at Hold. 

The firm's shares were up 8% since the start of the year.
